title: Dining with the Chef
desc: Chef Saito showcases his recipe for fukusa-sushi, a dish often served on special occasions, which features vibrant ingredients and seasoned rice wrapped within a delicate, thin omelette.

title: NHK Newsline
desc: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Document 72 Hours
desc: The only supermarket in Nishiwaga, Iwate Prefecture is stocked with a dazzling array of products. We spend three days meeting customers as they trek through heavy snowfall to visit this food hub.

title: NHK Newsline
desc: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Science View
desc: All animals need sleep. Exploring why and how sleep has evolved across the animal kingdom. Also, meeting the developer of the AI Suitcase that helps the visually impaired walk more freely.

title: NHK Newsline
desc: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Journeys in Japan
desc: A visit to Sugashima Island in Mie Prefecture reveals the legendary Ama, women who free dive for the fruits of the sea, showcasing a way of life intrinsically tied to the ocean.

title: NHK Newsline
desc: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Grand Sumo Highlights
desc: Sumo highlights from a range of tournaments that have taken place across the globe. Sumo is one of the most traditional Japanese sports and boasts a history going back more than 1500 years.

title: Dig More Japan!
desc: Explores how video games, far from being mere diversions, can assist in infrastructure maintenance and fundamentally alter professional lives.

title: Dining with the Chef Mini
desc: Featured recipe: Yaki-udon with Yuzukosho/ Learn about easy, delicious, and healthy cooking with Chef Rika in five minutes!

title: Sumopedia
desc: Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with information on the techniques, traditions and famous wrestlers of the past. The rules may be simple but there is a lot more to sumo than meets the eye.

desc: Comprehensive coverage of events in Japan and Asia. It explains the day's developments and what is behind them.

title: Direct Talk
desc: In 2020, Kitajima Masaaki published the world's first paper on how the spread of COVID-19 could be tracked by monitoring wastewater. He talks about making an impact on infectious disease control.

title: Century Signboards
desc: Explores Japan's oldest establishments that have survived and thrived for over 100 years. Behind them are stories of hardships and a skilful blend of tradition and innovation.

title: NHK World Japan
desc: A man strikes a pose in an iconic woodblock print by the artist Sharaku. We investigate who the mysterious figure is, examine what is he doing, and discuss why the artist wanted to commemorate the moment.

title: Japan in Focus
desc: Dive into Japan's current social landscape through a wide range of topics including initiatives tackling global challenges, domestic issues and emerging trends.

desc: The world is too divided to unite and tackle the issue of the Earth getting hotter every year. Alongside the COP30 discussions, we will explore possible ways to overcome this harsh reality.

title: Time-Lapse Japan
desc: Izumo, known as the home of the gods, fascinated Lafcadio Hearn, a writer from Ireland. With beautiful time-lapse footage, a look at his life in Japan and the region that captured his heart.
